content_en:
‘Amr bin Al-’As # left the court. Jeefer read the letter again and again. When he came to a particular sentence, he would stop.

“If both you brothers accept Islam, you will continue to rule over your land.”

This sentence was instrumental in turning him around completely. When ‘Amr bin Al-’As went to his court the following day, he adopted a harsh tone, but gradually he softened. Jeefer understood the delicate situation in which he found himself and agreed to accept Islam. He swore the oath of allegiance and entered the fold of Islam. When his subjects heard that he had accepted Islam, all of the inhabitants of ‘Oman also rapidly entered the fold of Islam. Thus, without any opposition, ‘Amr bin Al-’As .4 with his political sagacity played a pivotal role in making the people of ‘Oman lovers of Islam.

When the reinforcements reached their destination the question arose as to who would command the combined forces. ‘Amr bin Al-’As was of the opinion that he should be the Commander-in-Chief as the reinforcements had been sent at his request. Abu ‘Ubaidah bin AlJarrah - held the view that by rights he should be the Commanderin-Chief as noble Companions were in his army. But ‘Amr bin Al-’As 2

was a very determined man of strong will. It is a virtue in a successful general that once he adopts a position he does not agree to compromise on it. When Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah % saw that ‘Amr bin Al-'As was inflexible he accepted his command. He remembered the exhortation and advice of the Prophet 3 to avoid mutual conflicts in the battlefield.

‘Amr bin AlAs was very sensitive to the fact that the Prophet # had chosen him to command the Muslim army on this military expedition, and hence this honour was priceless. The other army had come at his request to help him, hence they should take part in the battle under him. And as the Commander-in-Chief, he felt it was also his responsibility to lead the prayers.

When ‘Amr bin AlAs 4s, commanding the Mujahideen, reached the battlefield where the enemy was encamped to oppose them, he immediately launched a massive attack; it was as if they did not know what had hit them. They had no time to organise themselves. Many of them were slain and the rest were forced to run for their lives. He warned the Mujahideen strictly not to chase the fleeing enemy.

It was a bitter winter, but Amr bin Al-’As 4% did not allow his men to light any fires to warm themselves. He issued strict orders that no fires were to be lit. ‘Umar bin Khattab -43 was furious at these restrictions, but Abu Bakr Siddiq calmed him down saying that the Prophet 38 must have had his own reasons for appointing ‘Amr bin Al-‘As 4 as Commander-in-Chief. He must have seen some virtue in him to honour him thus.

The Mujahideen insisted that the victorious army have the right to chase the enemy and completely decimate him and finish him off. But the Commander-in-Chief announced that anyone who disobeyed his orders would be severely punished. He issued a serious warning to the troops against pursuing the enemy. After ‘Amr bin Al-’As accepted Islam, this was his first expedition as Commander and he returned after gaining a major triumph. When ‘Amr bin Al-’As 6 gave the Prophet 3 an account of the Battle he expressed great happiness. Then he asked him affectionately why he had prevented the Mujahideen from chasing the fleeing enemy. He answered that he

had feared that the Mujahideen, while chasing the enemy might have gone too far into enemy territory and got killed. Since some precious lives could have been destroyed he had issued strict orders against pursuing the enemy. In fact it is the duty of a good general to look after the security and safety of his men.

Then the Prophet 38 asked him why he did not allow his men to warm themselves in the cold by lighting fires. ‘Amr bin Al-‘As answered that he had forbidden them because the enemy by the light of the fires would have come to know that their numbers were much smaller in comparison. If they had come to know this they could possibly have attacked again. He said that it was certainly very cold and he too felt liked lighting a fire, but he felt it was too risky to do so. The Prophet 38 was happy to hear his logical reasons.

In modern ages too, during war there are official announcements forbidding people to light fires. This concept of a blackout was first introduced by ‘Amr bin Al-’As 4 who was known for his military sagacity and foresight. Nobody can question the benefits of this procedure during war. And ’Amr bin Al-’As 4 fulfilled his duty as Commander to perfection in this first expedition of his after accepting Islam.

Another duty of a successful commander is to gauge the strength of the enemy and compare their respective numbers. If necessary he can call up reinforcements without any hesitation. As soon as ‘Amr bin Al- ‘As -% realised that he was outnumbered he got in touch with the capital, Al-Madinah, and asked for additional troops.

An excellent Commander also understands the responsibilities of his position towards the lives of the men under his command; he should do his utmost for their safety and security. That was the reason why ‘Amr bin AlAs 4 during this military expedition was very cautious in all the steps that he took. He would travel in darkness at night and would rest his army during the daylight hours in some obscure place; thus none could know that he was on the move or even guess his whereabouts. ‘Amr bin Al-’As e had the qualities of political insight, courage, bravery, firmness of resolve, farsightedness and an ability to make quick decisions without faltering. And all these qualities he

possessed to a very high degree of excellence. It was because of these virtues that in the Battle of Salasal he gained resounding victory. The Prophet 38 was so pleased with his triumph that he said:

“O ‘Amr! get ready for further victories.”

Doubtless these words uttered by the blessed tongue of the Prophet %, were treasured with pride to the end of life of ‘Amr bin Al-’As as they acknowledged wholeheartedly the military services rendered by him.

The Prophet 3 lived a full life and at the age of sixty-three he passed away to meet his Creator. His closest Companions, the respected Companions who were willing to give their lives for him, were totally devastated by grief. A death pall of silence overshadowed the life of all around him.

Overcome by his deep love for the Prophet 38, ‘Umar Farooq # raged and said that if anyone said the Prophet 4 had died he would chop off their head. Abu Bakr Siddiq < in order to control the volatile situation displayed exemplary restraint and in grief-stricken tines told the Companions:

“For those who worshipped the Prophet 38, he is dead, but for those who worship the One True Allah, doubtless He is alive and He will never die. Please be patient, for panic will not help or benefit us. We will move forward on the path shown to us by the Prophet 3. And the system given to us by him will continue.”

It was a very critical and testing moment in time for the Companions. Those tribes who had newly entered into the fold of Islam were turning, against it, and each one started to move in different directions with contrary viewpoints. Some said that the Prophet 38 was not a true prophet; if he had been a true prophet he would not have died. Some said they would pray but would'not pay zakah, Thus each tried to go

'! Man's love of worldly wealth! Zakét is one of the five pillars of Islam, and anyone who goes against the basic teachings will be considered an apostate. (Translator’s note).

his owned way. Some people tried to take advantage of the situation and declared themselves to be prophets. Perfidy, mischief, revolt apostasy - all raised their heads and it seemed as If there was a sudden assault from all sides. In order to and crush these multifarious evils, Abu Bakr Siddig # displayed great courage and determination. He organised eleven different armies of Muslims, each with its own banner and with its own commander and sent them in different directions to cut off the heads of this many-headed monster of rebellion and discord.

Khalid bin Waleed was sent to conquer Talihah bin Khawaled to the area of Bazakhah. After defeating him he had orders to proceed to the area of Bataéh to crush Malik bin Naweera who was creating problems there. ‘Ikrimah bin Abu Jahl 4 was sent to war against Musailmah Kaththab and ‘Amr bin Al-’As 46 had to deal with the tribe of Qadha’ah. Sharjeel bin Hasanah was told to first meet up with ‘Ikrimah bin Abu Jahl 4 and crush Musailmah Kaththab. When he was free of that problem he had to proceed to Hadhramaut, to attack the Kundah tribe who were creating trouble there. Suwaid bin Mugran was told to fight against the Tahamah people who settled in Yemen.
content_id:
'Amr bin Al-'As # meninggalkan istana. Jeefer membaca surat itu berulang-ulang. Ketika sampai pada kalimat tertentu, ia berhenti.

"Jika kalian berdua menerima Islam, kalian akan tetap berkuasa atas tanah kalian."

Kalimat ini berperan besar dalam mengubah sikapnya sepenuhnya. Ketika 'Amr bin Al-'As datang ke istananya keesokan harinya, ia bersikap keras, tetapi secara bertahap melunak. Jeefer memahami situasi rumit yang dihadapinya dan setuju menerima Islam. Ia mengucapkan sumpah kesetiaan dan masuk ke dalam Islam. Ketika rakyatnya mendengar bahwa ia telah menerima Islam, seluruh penduduk 'Oman juga dengan cepat memasuki Islam. Dengan demikian, tanpa perlawanan apa pun, 'Amr bin Al-'As .4 dengan kecerdasan politiknya memainkan peran penting dalam menjadikan penduduk 'Oman pecinta Islam.

Ketika bala bantuan tiba di tujuan, muncul pertanyaan tentang siapa yang akan memimpin pasukan gabungan. 'Amr bin Al-'As berpendapat bahwa ia seharusnya menjadi Panglima Tertinggi karena bala bantuan dikirim atas permintaannya. Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah - berpandangan bahwa sudah sepantasnya ia menjadi Panglima Tertinggi karena para sahabat mulia berada dalam pasukannya. Namun 'Amr bin Al-'As 2

adalah seorang yang sangat bertekad dan berkemauan kuat. Merupakan suatu keutamaan bagi seorang jenderal yang sukses bahwa begitu ia mengambil sikap, ia tidak mau berkompromi. Ketika Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah % melihat bahwa 'Amr bin Al-'As tidak dapat digoyahkan, ia menerima kepemimpinannya. Ia mengingat nasihat dan pesan Nabi ﷺ untuk menghindari perselisihan di medan perang.

'Amr bin Al-'As sangat menyadari bahwa Nabi # telah memilihnya untuk memimpin pasukan Muslim dalam ekspedisi militer ini, dan karenanya kehormatan ini tak ternilai harganya. Pasukan lain datang atas permintaannya untuk membantunya, maka mereka harus ikut bertempur di bawah komandonya. Dan sebagai Panglima Tertinggi, ia merasa sudah menjadi tanggung jawabnya pula untuk mengimami salat.

Ketika 'Amr bin Al-'As 4s, memimpin para Mujahidin, tiba di medan perang tempat musuh berkemah untuk menghadapi mereka, ia segera melancarkan serangan besar-besaran; seolah-olah mereka tidak tahu apa yang menimpa mereka. Mereka tidak punya waktu untuk mengorganisir diri. Banyak dari mereka yang terbunuh dan sisanya terpaksa lari menyelamatkan diri. Ia memperingatkan para Mujahidin dengan tegas untuk tidak mengejar musuh yang melarikan diri.

Saat itu musim dingin yang sangat keras, tetapi 'Amr bin Al-'As 4% tidak mengizinkan anak buahnya menyalakan api untuk menghangatkan diri. Ia mengeluarkan perintah tegas bahwa tidak boleh ada api yang dinyalakan. 'Umar bin Khattab -43 sangat marah dengan pembatasan ini, tetapi Abu Bakr Siddiq menenangkannya dengan mengatakan bahwa Nabi ﷺ pasti memiliki alasan tersendiri untuk menunjuk 'Amr bin Al-'As 4 sebagai Panglima Tertinggi. Beliau pasti melihat suatu keutamaan pada dirinya sehingga memberinya kehormatan tersebut.

Para Mujahidin bersikeras bahwa pasukan yang menang berhak mengejar musuh dan menghancurkannya secara total. Namun sang Panglima Tertinggi mengumumkan bahwa siapa pun yang melanggar perintahnya akan dihukum berat. Ia mengeluarkan peringatan serius kepada pasukan agar tidak mengejar musuh. Setelah 'Amr bin Al-'As menerima Islam, ini adalah ekspedisi pertamanya sebagai Panglima dan ia kembali dengan meraih kemenangan besar. Ketika 'Amr bin Al-'As 6 menyampaikan laporan tentang pertempuran tersebut kepada Nabi ﷺ, beliau mengungkapkan kegembiraan yang besar. Kemudian beliau bertanya kepadanya dengan penuh kasih sayang mengapa ia melarang para Mujahidin mengejar musuh yang melarikan diri. Ia menjawab bahwa ia

khawatir para Mujahidin, saat mengejar musuh, mungkin masuk terlalu jauh ke wilayah musuh dan terbunuh. Karena nyawa-nyawa berharga bisa saja hilang, ia mengeluarkan perintah tegas untuk tidak mengejar musuh. Sesungguhnya sudah menjadi kewajiban seorang jenderal yang baik untuk menjaga keselamatan dan keamanan anak buahnya.

Kemudian Nabi ﷺ bertanya kepadanya mengapa ia tidak mengizinkan anak buahnya menghangatkan diri di udara dingin dengan menyalakan api. 'Amr bin Al-'As menjawab bahwa ia melarang mereka karena musuh, dengan cahaya api, akan mengetahui bahwa jumlah mereka jauh lebih sedikit dibandingkan musuh. Jika mereka mengetahui hal ini, mereka mungkin akan menyerang kembali. Ia mengatakan bahwa udara memang sangat dingin dan ia pun ingin menyalakan api, tetapi ia merasa terlalu berisiko untuk melakukannya. Nabi ﷺ senang mendengar alasan-alasannya yang logis.

Di zaman modern pun, selama perang ada pengumuman resmi yang melarang orang menyalakan api. Konsep pemadaman lampu ini pertama kali diperkenalkan oleh 'Amr bin Al-'As 4 yang dikenal dengan kecerdasan dan pandangan jauh ke depannya dalam bidang militer. Tidak ada yang dapat mempertanyakan manfaat prosedur ini selama perang. Dan 'Amr bin Al-'As 4 menjalankan tugasnya sebagai Panglima dengan sempurna dalam ekspedisi pertamanya setelah menerima Islam.

Tugas lain dari seorang panglima yang sukses adalah mengukur kekuatan musuh dan membandingkan jumlah masing-masing. Jika perlu, ia dapat memanggil bala bantuan tanpa ragu. Begitu 'Amr bin Al-'As -% menyadari bahwa jumlahnya lebih sedikit, ia segera menghubungi ibu kota, Al-Madinah, dan meminta tambahan pasukan.

Seorang Panglima yang unggul juga memahami tanggung jawab kedudukannya terhadap nyawa orang-orang di bawah komandonya; ia harus berusaha semaksimal mungkin untuk keselamatan dan keamanan mereka. Itulah alasan mengapa 'Amr bin Al-'As 4 selama ekspedisi militer ini sangat berhati-hati dalam setiap langkah yang diambilnya. Ia bergerak dalam kegelapan di malam hari dan mengistirahatkan pasukannya di siang hari di tempat-tempat tersembunyi; sehingga tidak ada yang tahu bahwa ia sedang bergerak atau bahkan bisa menebak keberadaannya. 'Amr bin Al-'As e memiliki kualitas wawasan politik, keberanian, keperkasaan, keteguhan tekad, pandangan jauh ke depan, dan kemampuan mengambil keputusan cepat tanpa ragu. Dan semua kualitas ini ia

miliki dengan tingkat keunggulan yang sangat tinggi. Karena keutamaan-keutamaan inilah dalam Pertempuran Salasal ia meraih kemenangan gemilang. Nabi ﷺ sangat senang dengan kemenangannya sehingga beliau bersabda:

"Wahai 'Amr! Bersiaplah untuk kemenangan-kemenangan selanjutnya."

Tidak diragukan lagi, kata-kata yang diucapkan oleh lisan mulia Nabi ﷺ ini dijaga dengan penuh kebanggaan hingga akhir hayat 'Amr bin Al-'As karena kata-kata itu mengakui sepenuhnya jasa-jasa militer yang ia berikan.

Nabi ﷺ menjalani kehidupan yang penuh dan pada usia enam puluh tiga tahun beliau wafat menemui Penciptanya. Para sahabat terdekatnya, para sahabat terhormat yang rela mengorbankan nyawa mereka untuknya, benar-benar hancur oleh kesedihan. Keheningan duka menyelimuti kehidupan semua orang di sekitarnya.

Diliputi oleh cinta yang mendalam kepada Nabi ﷺ, 'Umar Farooq # marah dan berkata bahwa jika ada yang mengatakan Nabi ﷺ telah wafat, ia akan memenggal kepala mereka. Abu Bakr Siddiq < demi mengendalikan situasi yang bergejolak menunjukkan pengendalian diri yang patut diteladani dan dengan nada penuh duka memberitahu para sahabat:

"Bagi mereka yang menyembah Nabi ﷺ, beliau telah wafat, tetapi bagi mereka yang menyembah Allah Yang Maha Esa, sesungguhnya Dia hidup dan tidak akan pernah mati. Bersabarlah, karena kepanikan tidak akan menolong atau bermanfaat bagi kita. Kita akan terus melangkah di jalan yang telah ditunjukkan kepada kita oleh Nabi ﷺ. Dan sistem yang telah diberikan beliau kepada kita akan terus berlanjut."

Itu adalah saat yang sangat kritis dan menguji bagi para sahabat. Suku-suku yang baru masuk Islam mulai berbalik menentangnya, dan masing-masing mulai bergerak ke arah yang berbeda dengan pandangan yang bertentangan. Sebagian berkata bahwa Nabi ﷺ bukanlah nabi yang benar; jika beliau nabi yang benar, beliau tidak akan wafat. Sebagian berkata mereka akan salat tetapi tidak mau membayar zakah. Demikianlah masing-masing berusaha untuk

'! Kecintaan manusia terhadap harta dunia! Zakah adalah salah satu dari lima rukun Islam, dan siapa pun yang menentang ajaran dasar ini akan dianggap murtad. (Catatan penerjemah).

menempuh jalannya sendiri. Beberapa orang mencoba memanfaatkan situasi dan mengaku diri mereka sebagai nabi. Pengkhianatan, fitnah, pemberontakan, kemurtadan - semuanya mengangkat kepalanya dan seolah-olah ada serangan mendadak dari segala penjuru. Untuk menumpas berbagai kejahatan ini, Abu Bakr Siddiq # menunjukkan keberanian dan keteguhan yang luar biasa. Ia mengorganisir sebelas pasukan Muslim yang berbeda, masing-masing dengan panji dan panglimanya sendiri, dan mengirim mereka ke berbagai arah untuk memotong kepala-kepala monster pemberontakan dan perpecahan yang berkepala banyak ini.

Khalid bin Waleed dikirim untuk menaklukkan Talihah bin Khawaled di wilayah Bazakhah. Setelah mengalahkannya, ia mendapat perintah untuk melanjutkan ke wilayah Bataeh untuk menumpas Malik bin Naweera yang menimbulkan masalah di sana. 'Ikrimah bin Abu Jahl 4 dikirim untuk berperang melawan Musailmah Kaththab dan 'Amr bin Al-'As 46 harus menghadapi suku Qadha'ah. Sharjeel bin Hasanah diperintahkan untuk terlebih dahulu bertemu dengan 'Ikrimah bin Abu Jahl 4 dan menumpas Musailmah Kaththab. Setelah selesai dari masalah itu, ia harus melanjutkan ke Hadhramaut, untuk menyerang suku Kundah yang menimbulkan masalah di sana. Suwaid bin Mugran diperintahkan untuk berperang melawan kaum Tahamah yang menetap di Yaman.
content_ar: ‘Amr bin Al-’As # left the court. Jeefer read the letter again and again. عندما he came to a particular sentence, he would stop. “إذا both you brothers accept الإسلام, you will continue to rule over your land.” This sentence was instrumental in turning him around completely. عندما ‘Amr bin Al-’As went to his court the following day, he adopted a harsh tone, but gradually he softened. Jeefer understood the delicate situation in which he found himself and agreed to accept الإسلام. He swore the oath of allegiance and entered the fold of الإسلام. عندما his subjects heard that he had accepted الإسلام, all of the inhabitants of ‘Oman أيضاً rapidly entered the fold of الإسلام. وهكذا, without any opposition, ‘Amr bin Al-’As .4 with his political sagacity played a pivotal role in making the people of ‘Oman lovers of الإسلام. عندما the reinforcements reached their destination the question arose as to who would command the combined forces. ‘Amr bin Al-’As was of the opinion that he should be the القائد-in-Chief as the reinforcements had been sent at his request. Abu ‘Ubaidah bin AlJarrah - held the view that by الحقوق he should be the القائدin-Chief as noble الصحابة were in his الجيش. But ‘Amr bin Al-’As 2 was a very determined man of strong will. It is a الفضيلة in a successful general that حين he adopts a position he does not agree to compromise on it. عندما Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah % saw that ‘Amr bin Al-'As was inflexible he accepted his command. He remembered the exhortation and advice of النبي ﷺ 3 to avoid mutual conflicts in the المعركةfield. ‘Amr bin AlAs was very sensitive to the fact that النبي ﷺ # had chosen him to command the مسلم الجيش on this military expedition, and ومن ثَم this honour was priceless. The other الجيش had come at his request to help him, ومن ثَم they should take part in the المعركة under him. And as the القائد-in-Chief, he felt it was أيضاً his المسؤولية to lead the الصلوات. عندما ‘Amr bin AlAs 4s, commanding the المجاهدين, reached the المعركةfield where the العدو was encamped to oppose them, he immediately launched a massive attack; it was as إذا they did not know what had hit them. They had no time to organise themselves. Many of them were slain and the rest were forced to run for their lives. He warned the المجاهدين strictly not to chase the fleeing العدو. It was a bitter winter, but Amr bin Al-’As 4% did not allow his men to light any fires to warm themselves. He issued strict orders that no fires were to be lit. ‘عمر bin Khattab -43 was furious at these restrictions, but أبو بكر Siddiq calmed him down saying that النبي ﷺ 38 must have had his own reasons for appointing ‘Amr bin Al-‘As 4 as القائد-in-Chief. He must have seen some الفضيلة in him to honour him وهكذا. The المجاهدين insisted that the victorious الجيش have the الحق to chase the العدو and completely decimate him and finish him off. But the القائد-in-Chief announced that anyone who disobeyed his orders would be severely punished. He issued a serious warning to the القوات against pursuing the العدو. بعد ‘Amr bin Al-’As accepted الإسلام, this was his أولاً expedition as القائد and he returned بعد gaining a major triumph. عندما ‘Amr bin Al-’As 6 gave النبي ﷺ 3 an account of the المعركة he expressed great happiness. Then he asked him affectionately why he had prevented the المجاهدين from chasing the fleeing العدو. رد that he had feared that the المجاهدين, بينما chasing the العدو might have gone أيضاً far into العدو territory and got killed. بما أن some precious lives could have been destroyed he had issued strict orders against pursuing the العدو. In fact it is the الواجب of a الخير general to look بعد the security and safety of his men. Then النبي ﷺ 38 asked him why he did not allow his men to warm themselves in the cold by lighting fires. ‘Amr bin Al-‘As answered that he had forbidden them لأن the العدو by the light of the fires would have come لمعرفة that their numbers were much smaller in comparison. إذا they had come لمعرفة this they could possibly have attacked again. قال that it was certainly very cold and he أيضاً felt liked lighting a fire, but he felt it was أيضاً risky to do لذا. النبي ﷺ 38 was happy to hear his logical reasons. In modern ages أيضاً, خلال war there are official announcements forbidding people to light fires. This concept of a blackout was أولاً introduced by ‘Amr bin Al-’As 4 who was known for his military sagacity and foresight. Nobody can question the benefits of this procedure خلال war. And ’Amr bin Al-’As 4 fulfilled his الواجب as القائد to perfection in this أولاً expedition of his بعد accepting الإسلام. Another الواجب of a successful القائد is to gauge the القوة of the العدو and compare their respective numbers. إذا necessary he can call up reinforcements without any hesitation. As soon as ‘Amr bin Al- ‘As -% realised that he was outnumbered he got in touch with the capital, Al-المدينة المنورة, and asked for additional القوات. An excellent القائد أيضاً understands the responsibilities of his position towards the lives of the men under his command; he should do his utmost for their safety and security. That was the العقل why ‘Amr bin AlAs 4 خلال this military expedition was very cautious in all the steps that he took. He would travel in darkness at night and would rest his الجيش خلال the daylight hours in some obscure place; وهكذا none could know that he was on the move or even guess his whereabouts. ‘Amr bin Al-’As e had the qualities of political insight, الشجاعة, bravery, firmness of resolve, farsightedness and an ability to make quick decisions without faltering. And all these qualities he possessed to a very high degree of excellence. His closest الصحابة, the respected الصحابة who were willing to give their lives for him, were totally devastated by الحزن. A الموت pall of silence overshadowed the الحياة of all around him. Overcome by his deep المحبة for النبي ﷺ 38, ‘عمر Farooq # raged and said that إذا anyone said النبي ﷺ 4 had died he would chop off their head. أبو بكر Siddiq < من أجل control the volatile situation displayed exemplary restraint and in الحزن-stricken tines told the الصحابة: “For those who worshipped النبي ﷺ 38, he is dead, but for those who worship the One True الله, doubtless He is alive and He will never die. Please be patient, for panic will not help or benefit us. We will move forward on the الطريق shown to us by النبي ﷺ 3. And the system given to us by him will continue.” It was a very البالغ الأهمية and الابتلاء moment in time for the الصحابة. Those tribes who had newly entered into the fold of الإسلام were turning, against it, and each one started to move in different directions with contrary viewpoints. Some said that النبي ﷺ 38 was not a true النبي; إذا he had been a true النبي he would not have died. Some said they would pray but would'not pay الزكاة, وهكذا each tried to go '! Some people tried to take advantage of the situation and declared themselves to be الأنبياء. Perfidy, mischief, revolt apostasy - all raised their heads and it seemed as إذا there was a sudden assault from all sides. من أجل and crush these multifarious evils, أبو بكر Siddig # displayed great الشجاعة and determination. He organised eleven different armies of المسلمون, each with its own banner and with its own القائد and sent them in different directions to cut off the heads of this many-headed monster of rebellion and discord.
